posts.author: rdvs34283
posts.title: At Princeton, the Honor Code is Dead. Reversing a policy set in place in 1893 when Princeton introduced its honor code, all in-person exams will now require proctors to keep an eye on students. The change came after significant numbers of students requested it after widespread cheating.

link_metadata.description: Princeton ends honor code tradition, adds proctoring due to AI-assisted cheating surge.
link_metadata.title: At Princeton, the Honor Code Didn't Survive ChatGPT
